fail_closed_reasons: analysis_sufficiency:The evidence is insufficient for providing complete authoritative policy details. While it identifies the specific policy action (Item 10.3) and general enforcement intent (eliminating minimums), it is an advocacy letter predating the final adoption and lacks the specific effective dates and detailed enforcement scope of the enacted ordinance.

key_points: Policy Action: Adoption of City Council Item 10.3, 'Adopting a Comprehensive Parking Policy and TDM Ordinance,' scheduled for June 14, 2022.
The proposal includes eliminating parking minimums and basing parking provision on market demand rather than regulatory mandates.
Enforcement Scope: Implicates new construction, residential buildings, and developments near transit corridors.
Rationale: The policy aims to decrease housing costs (citing a 17% increase due to parking requirements) and help meet carbon neutrality goals by 2030.
Effective Date: The text provides the meeting date (June 14, 2022) for the vote but does not specify the effective date of the ordinance itself post-adoption.
analysis.sufficiency_state: The evidence is insufficient for providing complete authoritative policy details. While it identifies the specific policy action (Item 10.3) and general enforcement intent (eliminating minimums), it is an advocacy letter predating the final adoption and lacks the specific effective dates and detailed enforcement scope of the enacted ordinance.
analysis.summary: A letter dated June 13, 2022, from various advocacy groups to the San Jose City Council urging the adoption of City Council Item 10.3, a Comprehensive Parking Policy and TDM Ordinance, aimed at eliminating parking minimums to reduce housing costs and greenhouse gas emissions.
